family.choir: Seattle Pro Musica has completed their 2025-26 season auditions, but email auditions@seattlepromusica.org to join the mailing list for August 2026-27 season auditions. Requirements include music reading skills, choral experience, and ability to sing in foreign languages. Auditions involve vocalizing, sight-reading, ear training, and a prepared solo piece. Rehearsals are Tuesday evenings 6:30-9:30pm at Epiphany Parish Seattle. Seattle Choral Company holds auditions July-August for their 2025-26 season with performances including "Christmas in Latvia" and Bach/Handel masterworks. Contact them for 2026-27 season updates.
family.toddler: Sno-Isle Libraries offers regular toddler programming at both Edmonds and Lynnwood locations. Toddler Storytime (ages 18 months-3 years) runs Monday mornings at Lynnwood Library at 9:30am. Both libraries also offer Baby Storytime (newborn-18 months) and Preschool Storytime (ages 3-5). Imagine Children's Museum in Everett provides hands-on exhibits perfect for toddlers, including the Tinker Shop and indoor sock skating. Their Little Science Lab program visits Lynnwood Library on Monday, May 11 at 2:00pm. All Sno-Isle library storytimes feature stories, songs, rhymes and movement activities designed to develop early literacy skills.

intellectual_journals.findings: Flora Champy's essay 'The role of literature as the key to personal freedom' explores Marcel Proust's defense of reading as a unique form of communication that transcends moral instruction. Proust argues that books preserve indelible impressions of our surroundings, making time tangible and keeping past sensations alive. The essay contrasts Proust's view with John Ruskin's moralizing defense of reading, where Ruskin claimed books should train readers in disinterested reflection rather than serve as tools for social advancement. Proust's perspective emphasizes that reading provides access to th [TRUNCATED]

Maria Popova's essay 'Why You' presents a philosophical meditation on identity and existence, arguing that the self is 'a story of why you are you' - a selective retelling of chance events from the birth of the universe to the present moment. The piece draws on Iris Murdoch's observation that 'the self is a place of illusion' and connects this to Eastern philosophical traditions that view the dissolution of self-illusion as life's great achievement. The essay weaves together insights from Blaise Pascal, Philip Larkin, and James Joyce to explore how love serves as an affirmation of the improbab [TRUNCATED]

The essay 'Movements Need the Critical Thinking That AI Destroys' examines how outsourcing judgment to AI systems threatens democratic participation and emancipatory politics. Drawing on an MIT study showing reduced brain activity among regular chatbot users, the piece argues that AI represents a 'technologized form of subjectivity' that creates 'cognitive debt' - short-term efficiency gains at the cost of long-term cognitive capacity. The essay connects this to Kant's 1784 essay 'What Is Enlightenment?' which defined emancipation as freeing oneself from 'self-incurred immaturity' by using one [TRUNCATED]

Will Alden's 'Waiting for Day Zero' provides an intimate portrait of Iranian opposition figures in Los Angeles, focusing on the Constitutionalist Party of Iran's Easter Sunday picnic at Lake Balboa Park. The piece captures the surreal atmosphere of exile politics, where supporters of former crown prince Reza Pahlavi gather with 'THANK YOU BIBI & TRUMP' posters while sharing traditional Persian foods and listening to EDM tracks like 'Ayatollah Is Dead.' The essay explores the complex dynamics of diaspora politics, revealing how LA's 'Tehrangeles' community maintains hope for regime change while [TRUNCATED]

A major investigative report reveals that over 300 protesters arrested during Trump's immigration sweeps have seen their cases collapse under scrutiny. The investigation, conducted with FRONTLINE, found that federal agents routinely accused dissenters of domestic terrorism, but more than a third of cases were dismissed when video footage debunked officer statements. The report documents how ICE and CBP used conspiracy charges typically reserved for drug traffickers against protesters like Alejandro Orellana, a Marine veteran whose case fell apart within weeks. The investigation exposes a patte [TRUNCATED]

wearable_ai.findings:
**Friend AI Pendant** (friend.com) - The $99 voice-first AI companion from Avi Schiffmann's team has raised $7.9M total funding, with recent $5.4M seed led by Pace Capital. Worn as a necklace, it listens continuously and offers conversational support via voice messages. Currently shipping with 6-person team based in SF/India/UK.

**Omi** (omi.me) - Formerly "Friend Waitlist," this $2.7M-funded wea [TRUNCATED]

**MagicSchool AI** (magicschool.ai) - Dominant platform with 7M+ educators, $62.4M raised (Series B led by Valor Equity Partners). 224 employees (+138% YoY) across 16 countries. Offers comprehensive AI tools for lesson planning, assessments, IEPs, and differentiation. Recent launch of "AiOS" - AI Operating System for schools.

**Diffit** (diffit.me) - Berkeley-based 20-person team (+39% YoY growth [TRUNCATED]

**Nirva** (nirva.life) - AI jewelry that "journals your life" - 4-person SF team building privacy-first wearable for 24/7 emotional tracking and life coaching. Won 6 awards at CES 2026, backed by South Park Commons. Features multimodal sensing and "living library of your life."

**LumiLife** (lumilife.online) - German smart wearable ecosystem (watch/ring/glasses) with AI-powered app for personal s [TRUNCATED]

triadic_ontology.findings: Two fresh theoretical papers published 15 April 2026 on Zenodo advance the triadic-ontology program beyond the earlier Routledge volume.  “The Triadic Closure of Existence: Co-Emergence of Binding, Transformation, and Relational Readout” argues that observable reality is impossible unless three functional roles—stable binding, temporal transformation, and relational readout—phase-lock simultaneously, turning the old “three fundamental forces” trope into a strict structural imperative rather than an anthropic coincidence.  Companion piece “Mathematical Foundations of Triadic Closure and Recursive Computational Ontologies” supplies the formal machinery, unifying the Triadic Closure (“First Braid”) model with the octonionic Kosmoplex and the Nexus Recursive Harmonic Framework; it recasts particles and constants as runtime artifacts of an unbounded recursive computation, derives Standard-Model parameters via the Octonionic Ballot Matrix Transform, and predicts a controversial 33 Hz univers [TRUNCATED]

ai_systems.findings: Multi-agent AI systems have crossed a critical threshold in 2026, moving from experimental prototypes to fully autonomous research and development pipelines. Google Cloud AI Research's PaperOrchestra framework demonstrates this leap by orchestrating five specialized agents that can transform raw experimental data into submission-ready LaTeX manuscripts, making approximately 6,070 LLM calls per paper while handling literature reviews, figure generation, and citation verification autonomously. The Auton Agentic AI Framework, detailed in a February arXiv paper, provides a principled architecture for these systems, introducing a strict separation between declarative Cognitive Blueprints and Runtime Engines to enable cross-language portability and formal auditability. In astrophysics research, the Cmbagent system has proven that semi-autonomous agent workflows can compete with expert solutions, achieving first-place results in the FAIR Universe Weak Lensing Uncertainty Challenge through hum [TRUNCATED]

uap.findings: Congress is accelerating UAP disclosure through multiple fronts. The House Task Force on Declassification, led by Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, continues pressing the Pentagon for 46 withheld UAP videos and broader transparency. A classified March 25 SCIF briefing—described by Rep. Eric Burlison as "the most bipartisan topic in Washington"—sparked controversy following the mysterious February disappearance of retired Air Force General Neil McCasland, who oversaw classified aerospace programs at Wright-Patterson. The FY2026 NDAA mandates expanded congressional briefings on UAP intercepts since 2004 and requires the All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) to account for all UAP-related classification guides. President Trump's February directive ordering federal agencies to release UAP/alien files has triggered interagency coordination, though former officials like Christopher Mellon caution that genuine disclosure requires systematic declassification review. Most significantly, Sen. Schume [TRUNCATED]
